channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: BYU hopes to finish strong after 6-0 start
item.description: PROVO, Utah (ABC4 Sports) - From being picked 13th in the Big 12 Conference, to be ranked 13th in the nation, BYU is off to a 6-0 start for just the sixth time in school history without any superstars or sure-fire NFL prospects. In fact, 16 different players have scored touchdowns this year.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Too many men: No. 2 Oregon purposely induced late penalty in win over No. 4 Ohio State
item.description: EUGENE, Ore. (AP) — No. 2 Oregon had 12 men on the field late its 32-31 win over No. 4 Ohio State, with the resulting penalty causing precious seconds to wind off the clock. Turns out, it was on purpose.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NFL fans rip refs as Bills hold on for crucial divisional win over Jets
item.description: The Buffalo Bills edged the New York Jets, 23-20, to win their Monday night matchup but it was the officials who roiled fans during the game.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 76ers' free agent pickup Paul George leaves preseason win with hyperextended left knee
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Philadelphia 76ers' prized free-agent acquisition Paul George left Monday's preseason game against Atlanta with a hyperextended left knee. There was no immediate update how much time George might miss following his injury.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Nevada could be latest to forfeit SJSU volleyball match amidst transgender lawsuit
item.description: SAN JOSE, Calif. (KRON) -- The University of Nevada, Reno (UNR) could be the latest team to forfeit a women's volleyball match against San Jose State University.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Tolentino, KRON4

item.title: Jets' Aaron Rodgers throws incredible Hail Mary TD pass before halftime vs Bills
item.description: New York Jets quarterback Aaron Rodgers launched up a prayer before halftime against the Buffalo Bills on Monday night and it was answered.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Weekend full of injuries in college football leaves plenty of playoff contenders scrambling
item.description: As the college football season reaches its midway point, plenty of playoff contenders suddenly find themselves dealing with season-ending injuries to notable players.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Kershaw says he plans to return to Dodgers for 18th season in 2025
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Clayton Kershaw says he plans to return to the Los Angeles Dodgers for his 18th season next year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Chargers coach Jim Harbaugh wearing heart monitor, taking blood thinner after irregular heartbeat
item.description: EL SEGUNDO, Calif. (AP) — Leave it to Jim Harbaugh to call an audible when addressing his health. The Los Angeles Chargers coach wasn’t supposed to do his Monday news conference because he was undergoing medical testing after he experienced an irregular heartbeat during Sunday’s 23-16 win at Denver.
item.dc:creator: JOE REEDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rizzo at first base for Yankees in ALCS opener against Cleveland, 16 days after fracturing fingers
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Anthony Rizzo was back in the New York Yankees' lineup for Monday night's American League Championship Series opener, just 16 days after fracturing a pair of fingers when hit by a pitch.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cast aside by Dallas Stars, first-round pick has impressed Sharks
item.description: NHL: In and out of the Dallas Stars' lineup last season, Ty Dellandrea could have a steady role with the San Jose Sharks.
item.dc:creator: Curtis Pashelka, The Mercury News

item.title: Islanders coach Patrick Roy returns to Colorado and the building where his retired number hangs
item.description: DENVER (AP) — New York Islanders coach Patrick Roy stepped onto the ice at Ball Arena and briefly glanced toward the rafters. Yep, his familiar No. 33 was still up there with all the other banners representing retired Colorado Avalanche numbers.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Will they play or not? Another twist in San Jose State transgender women’s volleyball player controversy
item.description: Nevada officials say their players' decision not to play San Jose State, " does not represent the position of the University. The University intends to move forward with the match as scheduled, and the players may choose not to participate in the match on the day of the contest.
item.dc:creator: Laurence Miedema, The Mercury News

item.title: Review: ‘King James’ is a basketball play that’s about a lot more than basketball
item.description: Rajiv Joseph's buddy drama 'King James' at TheatreWorks Silicon Valley turns out to be a sweeping look at what a true friendship really entails.
item.dc:creator: Chad Jones, Correspondent, The Mercury News

item.title: Los Angeles Rams implementing new AI technology during football games
item.description: The technologically advanced home of the Los Angeles Rams is about to have a new way for fans to track players’ statistics from their seats during the game.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: MLB playoffs averaging 3.33 million viewers through division series, an 18% increase over last year
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Major League Baseball's postseason is averaging 3.33 million viewers going into the league championship series, an 18% increase over last year's average of 2.82 million. The division series averaged 3.56 million viewers for 18 games, a 14% increase from last year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Los Angeles Clippers to make Intuit Dome debut Monday
item.description: For the first time in one quarter of a century, the Los Angeles Clippers will play a home game in their own, exclusive arena on Monday night. That's because the Intuit Dome, the $2 billion Inglewood arena, is now open seven years after it was first announced to be in the works.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Snag last-minute Yankees ALCS tickets for under $100
item.description: THE BRONX, N.Y. (PIX11) --- Yankees fans can snag some last-minute tickets to Monday night's playoff game for cheaper than a regular season game. The least expensive seats for Game 1 of the ALCS at Yankee Stadium were going for around $102 on StubHub, as of Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Mira Wassef, PIX 11

item.title: Shohei Ohtani stolen base streak ends at 36 in NLCS, caught for first time since July 22
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles Dodgers keep on winning in the playoffs, even without a bunch of home runs and stolen bases by Shohei Ohtani.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Dodgers tie postseason mark of 33 straight scoreless innings, top Mets 9-0 in NLCS opener
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — After spending the first seven years of his big league career back East, Jack Flaherty came home. He joined a winning team in the Los Angeles Dodgers and helped make a bit of playoff history.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bears TE Cole Kmet catches 2 of Caleb Williams' 4 TD passes, handles long snaps in 35-16 rout of Jaguars in London
item.description: WATCH: Jarrett Payton loves what he's seeing from the Bears' offense in the attached video from Monday's WGN Morning News. LONDON (AP) — Chicago Bears tight end Cole Kmet faced his biggest fear in football with no notice. In a snap, you might say.
item.dc:creator: KEN MAGUIRE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: AP Top 25: Oregon, Penn State move behind No. 1 Texas. Army, Navy both ranked for 1st time since '60
item.description: Oregon and Penn State each moved up a spot in The Associated Press college football poll on Sunday following thrilling wins in high-profile games, and Top 25 newcomers Navy and Army are in the rankings together for the first time since 1960.
item.dc:creator: ERIC OLSON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Bears dominate Jaguars in London, 35-16, to extend winning streak to 3 games going into bye week
item.description: The Bears capped off their London trip in style Sunday, routing the Jacksonville Jaguars, 35-16, at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium to extend their winning streak to three games and improve to 4-2 on the season.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: North Carolina football player dies from rare lung cancer at 23
item.description: UNC had honored Tylee Craft, 23, during Saturday's game. Coach Mack Brown, however, said he hadn't learned of Craft's death until after the game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Bears vs. Jaguars: Teams battling in early-morning tilt at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in London
item.description: UPDATE: The Bears and Jaguars have announced their inactives for Sunday's game. Inactives for the Bears are DB Jaquan Brisker, DB Tyrique Stevenson, OL Nate Davis, DL Zacch Pickens, WR Velus Jones Jr., DB Terrell Smith and DL Dominque Robinson.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: Kucherov scores go-ahead goal, two empty-netters to push Lightning past Hurricanes in season opener
item.description: Nikita Kucherov had a third-period hat trick to help the Tampa Bay Lightning beat the Carolina Hurricanes 4-1 in Friday night's season opener for both teams.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Be careful when buying Dodgers-Padres NLDS Game 5 tickets online, USAA says
item.description: Friday night will bring the biggest baseball game in Los Angeles in years as the San Diego Padres visit the Dodgers for a winner-take-all Game 5 in the National League Division Series. And the high stakes are bringing high ticket prices.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Giancarlo Stanton hits go-ahead homer in the eighth, Yankees beat Royals 3-2 in Game 3 of the ALDS
item.description: KANSAS CITY, Mo. (AP) — Giancarlo Stanton hit a go-ahead homer in the eighth inning amid a battle of bullpens, and the New York Yankees beat the Kansas City Royals 3-2 on Wednesday night in Game 3 of their AL Division Series at Kauffman Stadium.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PIX 11
